text: The majority of successful endurance athletes follow a pyramidal training intensity distribution with a high proportion of high-volume, low-intensity training.
population: nationally and internationally competitive endurance athletes

text: World-class athletes adopt a polarized training intensity distribution (significant proportions of both low-intensity and high-intensity training) during certain phases of the season.

Prospective randomized controlled studies demonstrate superior endurance-related adaptations from polarized training intensity distribution compared to threshold-focused or purely high-volume low-intensity training.
Threshold-focused training intensity distribution produces inferior endurance adaptations compared to polarized training in well-trained individuals.
Training intensity distribution varies across preparation, pre-competition, and competition phases, suggesting periodization of intensity balance across the season.
population: endurance athletes across disciplines and performance levels
